title: Topic: Urijah Faber's A1 Combat 34Urijah Faber's A1 Combat 34: Maximov vs. Wallace 2

posts.author: Tapology
posts.content: The official Tapology discussion thread for the event!

posts.author: AyyLmaonnaise
posts.content:
Predictions: 0 of 11 Winners, 0 Perfect, 0 Points
|
Tied for 1st with 184 others

Ding vs. Huseynov
Meng Ding, TKO, R2
I didn’t see the first fight but from the clip I saw it looked like Huseynov gassed out and quit, so idk why there needs to be a rematch. I like both of these guys so hopefully the winner ca get a dwcs shot. Neither are good but they’re both fun (except for that horrendous dwcs fight from ding)

Nkuta vs. Kawahara
Phumi Nkuta, TKO, R3
Didn’t expect Phumi to be here but this is cool to see. I know Phumi decision is a lock but I’m holding out hope he can find something to finally get a big shot. I’ve given up hope he ends up in the ufc but maybe something could happen. If not rizin would be a great choice. Phumi isn’t the most exciting and def is a little overrated but he’s still a great fighter at the end of the day

Maximov vs. Wallace
Buddy Wallace, DEC
Picked Maximov the first time but seeing him quit to a r1 eye poke tells me he’s still the same guy he was in ufc. Never liked him so let’s go buddy

Perez vs. Simmons
Jureall Simmons, TKO, R1
Even regional HW is cooked. These two are both awful

Sandoval vs. Escolta
Cameron Sandoval, DEC
I saw Talbott subbed this guy as an amateur so rooting for him

Souza vs. Juarez
Bruno Souza, DEC
Souza should take this fairly easily. I’ve never been impressed with Juarez and he’s looked terrible since the Delgado loss. Souza’s style is a very tough watch but he is a good fighter. Way too good for regionals but I also don’t think his style is that effective in the ufc either. He’d be a solid get for pfl

Borshchev vs. Nakashima
Viacheslav Borshchev, DEC
Slava’s ufc career ended in disaster but against regional level comp I think he can still compete. His striking is great but every other aspect of his mma game is terrible, and he seems a little physically washed now. Nakshima is old and not an amazing wrestler though so I think Slava can outstrike him. Wouldn’t be shocked if he’s just completely done and gets grinded out again though

Esembaev vs. Hanes
Yusuf Esembaev, TKO, R1
Great step up for Yusuf. He has looked amazing thus far, only 21 and destroying everyone. He has two spinning elbow kos and a sub over one of Talbott’s sparring partners. Hes shown a well rounded skillset and is super dynamic. I do wonder about cardio, especially since this other guy is a real fighter. He seems like the goods though, so I will back him
